metadata.title: Robust Object Detection in Adverse Weather Conditions: ECL-YOLOv11 for Automotive Vision Systems
metadata.description: This paper addresses the challenge of degraded object detection performance in adverse weather conditions such as fog, rain, and snow, which compromise safety in autonomous driving systems. It proposes ECL-YOLOv11, an enhanced detection framework integrating three modules: edge-enhancement for boundary retention, context-guided multi-scale fusion for improved small-object perception, and a lightweight detection head for real-time inference. The model achieves state-of-the-art accuracy (mAP@50: 62.7%, mAP@50–95: 40.5%) and real-time speed (237.8 FPS), outperforming baseline YOLOv11. Ablation studies and visualizations confirm robustness across weather and motion conditions, offering a reliable foundation for all-weather autonomous perception.
